meta.question: What timing requirements are formalized for the APS?
meta.finalAnswer:
No formal timing requirements for the APS (i.e., no rdf:type vocab:Duration%20constraint / vocab:Time%20constraint elements associated with APS-named containers such as “APS Performance Requirements” or “APS Notes”) were found in the RDF model using the available ownership/containment and constraint-linking patterns.

What I did find for APS were various APS-related views/packages/documents/activities (e.g., “APS Notes” and “APS Performance Requirements”), but the model data did not expose any connected formal Duration/Time constraint nodes under those elements via the query patterns available.

request.reason: The initial query returned no results, so the pattern for how timing constraints connect to the APS requirement likely differs from the assumed predicate path (e.g., different linking predicates or constraint representation). Next I will first locate the APS element and inspect its rdf:type and connected properties, then specifically map how it relates to requirements/constraints before extracting the timing expressions.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS")))
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: I found multiple APS-related elements, including “APS Notes” and the “APS Performance Requirements” view. The earlier query failed because it assumed the APS was itself a specific tmt:requirement with direct vocab:constraint connections. Next I will map properties for the most likely APS requirement container (“APS Performance Requirements”) to see how timing constraints are represented and linked.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS Performance Requirements")))
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
}
GROUP BY ?prop
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: No element/property triples matched the exact-named string “APS Performance Requirements” via vocab:NamedElement_name, likely due to encoding/case/spaces or because the relevant constraints are nested differently. Next I will broaden the mapping to any element whose name contains “APS Performance Requirements” or “APS Requirements” and then locate Duration/Time constraint nodes reachable from them.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?container ?containerName ?timingType ?timingName WHERE {
  ?container vocab:NamedElement_name ?containerName .
  FILTER(
    CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?containerName)), LCASE("aps performance requirements")) ||
    CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?containerName)), LCASE("aps requirements")) ||
    CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?containerName)), LCASE("aps notes"))
  )

  # find constraints anywhere owned/contained by the container
  ?container vocab:Element_ownedElement+ ?timing .
  ?timing rdf:type ?timingType .
  ?timing vocab:NamedElement_name ?timingName .

  FILTER(
    ?timingType IN (
      vocab:Duration%20constraint,
      vocab:Time%20constraint,
      vocab:Duration%20interval,
      vocab:Time%20interval
    )
    || CONTAINS(STR(?timingType), "constraint")
  )
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Across several exploration attempts, I could not locate any Duration/Time constraint elements that are directly owned/contained under APS-named containers. Given that the dataset may store the APS timing requirements as text in documents/views rather than as formal constraint nodes, I will report that no formal timing constraints (Duration/Time constraints) were found for APS in the RDF model using the available linkage patterns.

No formal timing requirements for the APS (i.e., no rdf:type vocab:Duration%20constraint / vocab:Time%20constraint elements associated with APS-named containers such as “APS Performance Requirements” or “APS Notes”) were found in the RDF model using the available ownership/containment and constraint-linking patterns.

What I did find for APS were various APS-related views/packages/documents/activities (e.g., “APS Notes” and “APS Performance Requirements”), but the model data did not expose any connected formal Duration/Time constraint nodes under those elements via the query patterns available.
